chapter_voice_summary: BG 2.20 closes block B with the chapter's didactic-corrective voice intact. The translation paragraph builds the lineage point with concrete factual cadence ('predates OAuth, predates HTTPS, predates web browsers' / 'predates Stripe, predates credit cards' / 'predates SMTP, predates personal computers') — declarative, not aspirational. The implication is operational ('audit your team's language for ownership claims that don't hold'). The voice neither bullies nor consoles; it informs. The block-B closure register feels right — the verse is the substrate doctrine's final naming, and the voice carries appropriate weight without escalating into solemnity. Sanskrit register markers (ajaḥ nityaḥ śāśvato 'yaṁ purāṇaḥ) used consistently with prior verses. No drift.
